hypothesis:
Based on the identified gap in the paper regarding distinguishing particles during severe overlap and the demonstrated success of full fine-tuning on synthetic data, I propose the following hypothesis:

**Hypothesis:** Training SAM-EM on synthetic LPTEM videos that systematically vary the degree and duration of particle overlap (quantified by overlap area fraction and temporal persistence) will enable the model to learn multi-object boundary disambiguation features that reduce identity switching events by at least 50% compared to the current model when tested on experimental LPTEM datasets containing high-density nanoparticle assemblies with frequent occlusions.
